data.title: Hello, world from Rust
data.excerpt: A blog post stored as a JSON file in @webhouse/cms and rendered by an Axum + Tokio application.
data.content:
## The setup

This post lives at `content/posts/hello-world.json`. **Axum reads it directly** via the `webhouse::Reader` struct — no API calls, no database, just `std::fs::read_to_string` and `serde_json`.

## Memory-safe by default

The whole reader is ~180 lines of Rust. No `unwrap()` panics in the happy path, all errors return `Result<T, WebhouseError>`. Path traversal protection via `canonicalize() + starts_with()`.

## Single static binary

`cargo build --release` produces a ~5 MB binary with zero runtime dependencies. Deploy it with `scp` and run.
data.author: Christian Broberg
